question_text: A bicycle pump has a cross-sectional area of 5 cm². When the pump handle is pushed down with a force of 50 N, what is the pressure exerted by the air inside the pump on the pump cylinder?
explanation: Pressure = Force ÷ Area. First convert 5 cm² to m²: 5 cm² = 5 × 10⁻⁴ m². Then: P = 50 N ÷ (5 × 10⁻⁴ m²) = 100,000 Pa. This shows how a moderate force can create high pressure when applied over a small area.
